properties.remark: EF0 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN NEAR OTT STANFORD RD. PATIO ROOF OFF OF FIRST HOUSE WAS PEELED BACK AND RIPPED OFF HALF OF THE ROOF OF THE HOUSE. PATIO ROOF WAS RIPPED OFF OF ANOTHER HOUSE ACROSS THE FIELD. A WELL HOUSE WAS DESTROYED. ONE SMALL PINE WAS SNAPPED IN HALF. MAX ESTIMATED WIND SPEED 75 MPH...MAX WIDTH 100 YARDS...AND PATH LENGTH WAS 200 YARDS.

A WEAK TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AND RIPPED SIDING AND PORTION OF THE ROOF OFF OF A METAIL BOAT STORAGE BUILDING...SNAPPED A COUPLE OF SMALL TREES...DESTROYED A STORAGE SHED AT RESIDENCE...AND MOVED A SHIPPING CONTAINER 30 FEET. PATH LENGTH APPROXIMATELY ONE QUARTER MILE AND PATH WIDTH 30 YARDS.

NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AND TRACKED THROUGH PORTIONS OF PRAIRIEVILLE. THE TORNADO INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN ALONG DUPLESSIS RD...TRACKED APPROXIMATELY 3 MILES BEFORE LIFTING AFTER CROSSING NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAYS 42 AND 930. SEVERAL HOMES RECEIVED SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE AND MANY TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. PATHWIDTH WAS APPROXIMATELY 50 YARDS. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED WAS 110 MPH.
SCATTERED TREES DOWN ACROSS PARISH. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

WEAK TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST SOUTH OF VARNADO ROAD AND TRACKED NORTH-NORTHWEST ACROSS NORTH CORBIN ROAD. A HOME LOST A PORTION OF THE ROOF AND SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED ALONG THE TRACK. THE TORNADO LIFTED SOUTHWEST OF NORTH CORBIN ELEMENTARY SCHOOL. PATH LENGTH WAS APPROXIMATELY 0.5 MILES...PATH WIDTH 50 YARDS...MAXIMUM TORNADO STRENGTH EF1 WITH ESTIMATED WINDS OF 100 MPH.

A NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM OBSERVED DAMAGE PRODUCED BY A TORNADO NORTHEAST OF MADISONVILLE. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE CLUB HOUSE IN TCHEFUNCTA COUNTRY CLUB ESTATES NEIGBORHOOD. THE TORNADO CONTINUED ON A PATH TO THE NORTHEAST DOWNING SEVERAL TREES AND PRODUCING MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO A COUPLE HOMES BEFORE LIFTING IN A HEAVILY WOODED AREA BEYOND THE NEIGHBORHOOD. THE TORNADO HAD A WIDTH OF APPROX 100 YDS AND PATH LENGTH OF ONE HALF MILE.

WEAK EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF SILVER SPRINGS DR AND ALLEN RD. IT MOVED NNW SNAPPING SEVERAL PINE TREES. SOME ROOF DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED TO ONE HOME WHERE PART OF A TREE HAD FALLEN THROUGH THE ROOF. THE TORNADO LIFTED AFTER CROSSING JARRELL RD. MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WIND SPEED 65 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH 25 YDS. ALONG JOHN LEE ROAD AND ROBERTS ROAD...A 30 FOOT CAMPER WAS FLIPPED...TREES WERE TWISTED AND TOPPLED...THE ROOF OF A SHED WAS BLOWN OFF...AND A FENCE ANCHORED IN CEMENT WAS BLOWN DOWN. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON JOHN LEE ROAD AND ROBERTS ROAD. TREES WERE TWISTED... A TRAVEL TRAILER WAS FLIPPED... AND SEVERAL HOMES LOST A PORTION OF THEIR ROOF. THE PATHWIDTH WAS 100 YARDS AND WAS ON THE GROUND FOR NEARLY 1 MILE. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED WAS 90 MPH.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG HWY 67 AT HWY 15. MOST OF THE DAMAGE WAS AT THE STARTING POINT WITH A LARGE SHED DOOR BLOWN IN CAUSING ROOF DAMAGE AND A PATIO ROOF LIFTED OFF. MOST OF THE REMAINDER OF THE DAMAGE WAS SHINGLES AND FENCES WITH AN END POINT IN COVENTRY ESTATES. THE PATHWIDTH WAS MEASURED AT 100 YARDS WITH DAMAGE TRACKED FOR AROUND 2 1/2 MILES. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED WAS 85 MPH.
